title: Fix connecting unpacked real arrays with opposite index ranges
body:
Compilation of the following module results in an error: `%Error: Input port connection 'i1' expected non-complex non-double pin connection in width check`
```SystemVerilog
module a(input real i1 [0:4]);
endmodule: a

module b(input real i2 [4:0]);
  a a_inst(.i1(i2));
endmodule: b
```

The module compiles successfully when both arrays have identical index ranges (e.g., [4:0] in both cases), but fails when one has [0:4] and the other [4:0].

Options: `--cc --exe testbench.cpp --timing -Wno-fatal +1800-2023ext+sv`

### Expected Behavior
`i1` and `i2` are both arrays with the same number of elements, so their connection should be valid regardless of index order

This issue occurs in `Verilator 5.030 2024-10-27 rev v5.030-45-g2cb1a8de7`

Found by a [verilog model](https://github.com/DepTyCheck/verilog-model) using [deptycheck](https://github.com/buzden/deptycheck)
